heading: Review of Subsidies Agreement
label: 3572 Review of Subsidies Agreement
notes: CodificationSection is comprised of . Subsec. (c)(4) of amended .section 282 of Pub. L. 103–465section 282 of Pub. L. 103–465section 2191 of this title

Uruguay Round Agreements: Entry Into ForceThe Uruguay Round Agreements, including the World Trade Organization Agreement and agreements annexed to that Agreement, as referred to in , entered into force with respect to the United States on . See note set out under .section 3511(d) of this titlesection 3511 of this titleJan. 1, 1995
amendments: CodificationSection is comprised of . Subsec. (c)(4) of amended .section 282 of Pub. L. 103–465section 282 of Pub. L. 103–465section 2191 of this title

children.text: to ensure that parts II and III of the Agreement on Subsidies and Countervailing Measures referred to in (hereafter in this section referred to as the “Subsidies Agreement”) are effective in disciplining the use of subsidies and in remedying the adverse effects of subsidies, andsection 3511(d)(12) of this title
to ensure that part IV of the Subsidies Agreement does not undermine the benefits derived from any other part of that Agreement.

children.heading: Specific objective
children.label: b Specific objective
children.text: The specific objective of the United States under this subchapter shall be to create a mechanism which will provide for an ongoing review of the operation of part IV of the Subsidies Agreement.

children.heading: Sunset of noncountervailable subsidies provisions
children.label: c Sunset of noncountervailable subsidies provisions
A
children.text: the Subsidies Committee determines to extend Articles 6.1, 8, and 9 of the Subsidies Agreement as in effect on the date on which the Subsidies Agreement enters into force or in a modified form, in accordance with Article 31 of such Agreement,

children.label: B
children.text: the President consults with the Congress in accordance with paragraph (2), and

children.label: C
children.text: an implementing bill is submitted and enacted into law in accordance with paragraphs (3) and (4).1See Codification note below.1

children.heading: Consultation with Congress before Subsidies Committee agrees to extend
children.label: 2 Consultation with Congress before Subsidies Committee agrees to extend
children.text: Before a determination is made by the Subsidies Committee to extend Articles 6.1, 8, and 9 of the Subsidies Agreement, the President shall consult with the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate regarding such extension.

children.label: I
children.text: a draft of an implementing bill,

children.label: II
children.text: a statement of any administrative action proposed to implement the extension, and

children.label: III
children.text: the supporting information described in subparagraph (C); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the implementing bill is enacted into law.

children.heading: Implementing bill
children.label: B Implementing bill
children.text: The implementing bill referred to in subparagraph (A) shall contain only those provisions that are necessary or appropriate to implement an extension of the provisions of section 1677(5B)(B), (C), (D), and (E) of this title as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of the implementing bill or as modified to reflect the determination of the Subsidies Committee to extend Articles 6.1, 8, and 9 of the Subsidies Agreement.

children.label: i
children.text: an explanation as to how the implementing bill and proposed administrative action will change or affect existing law; and

children.label: I
children.text: how the extension serves the interests of United States commerce, and

children.label: II
children.text: why the implementing bill and proposed administrative action is required or appropriate to carry out the extension.

children.heading: Report by the Trade Representative
children.label: 5 Report by the Trade Representative
children.text: Not later than the date referred to in section 1677(5B)(G)(i) of this title, the Trade Representative shall submit to the Congress a report setting forth the provisions of law which were enacted to implement Articles 6.1, 8, and 9 of the Subsidies Agreement and should be repealed or modified if such provisions are not extended.

children.heading: Review of operation of Subsidies Agreement
children.label: d Review of operation of Subsidies Agreement

children.text: the effectiveness of part II of the Subsidies Agreement in disciplining the use of subsidies which are prohibited under Article 3 of the Agreement,
the effectiveness of part III and, in particular, Article 6.1 of the Subsidies Agreement, in remedying the adverse effects of subsidies which are actionable under the Agreement, and

children.label: A
children.text: the extent to which WTO member countries have cooperated in reviewing and improving the operation of part IV of the Subsidies Agreement,

children.label: B
children.text: the extent to which the provisions of Articles 8.4 and 8.5 of the Subsidies Agreement have been effective in identifying and remedying violations of the conditions and criteria described in Article 8.2 of the Agreement, and

children.label: C
children.text: the extent to which the provisions of Article 9 of the Subsidies Agreement have been effective in remedying the serious adverse effects of subsidy programs described in Article 8.2 of the Agreement.
